articles.title: Lebanese army to control 'pilot zone' in area occupied by Israeli forces, US official says
articles.summary: Lebanese army to control 'pilot zone' in area occupied by Israeli forces, US official says The first so-called “pilot zone” in Lebanon, in which the Lebanese army will assume control of an area currently occupied by Israeli forces, will launch in a “matter of days”, with further zones being mapped out and planned, a US official said on Thursday. According to the official, Lebanon-Israel talks have moved to the implementation stage of the ceasefire framework.  This comes after Netanyahu said on Thursday that Israeli forces would stay in Lebanon for “as long as the need requires”.

articles.title: Israel ready to attack Iran ‘with even greater force’ if needed, says defence minister
articles.summary: Israel ready to attack Iran ‘with even greater force’ if needed, says defence minister Israeli Defence Minister Israel Katz said on Thursday that his country was prepared to resume military operations against Iran for “a third time if necessary”. “The army is ready and on alert for a resumption of fighting, in order to regain air superiority and strike again… in Iran, to eliminate threats, including a third time if necessary,” Katz said at a military ceremony, adding, “If we have to go back, we will go back, with even greater force.”
